target.title: fix #91047: Plugin session-extension registry not pinned; sessions.pluginPatch fails after agent/subagent plugin-load churn
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: startup-registered plugin session extensions could disappear from `sessions.pluginPatch` after later agent/subagent plugin-load churn replaced the active plugin registry.
- Solution: add a pinned session-extension registry surface alongside the existing HTTP-route and channel pinned surfaces.
- What changed: `sessions.pluginPatch` and session row extension projection now read the session-extension surface; gateway startup/reload and standalone loaders pin/release that surface with the plugin runtime lifecycle.
- What did NOT change: public plugin SDK API, config schema, session store schema, gateway protocol params/defaults, and `sessions.pluginPatch` wire behavior are not changed; external plugin bundle loading and UI descriptor behavior are out of scope and intentionally left unchanged.
- Why it matters / User impact: plugin-owned session UI/state can keep accepting patches after runtime registry churn instead of failing with `unknown plugin session extension`, preserving the session-state lifecycle contract for registered extensions.
- Fixes #91047

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A plugin can register a session extension during startup, then later agent/subagent plugin-load churn can replace the active registry. Before this patch, `sessions.pluginPatch` and session row projection could read only the mutable active registry and reject or hide the startup-owned extension. The latest head also fixes the final empty-pinned case: an intentionally empty startup session-extension surface remains authoritative and does not fall through to later active extensions.

**Real environment tested:** Linux local OpenClaw worktree for PR #91208 at commit `cbb672ba60134113424ab37b648840bfbb3b655a`. Node 22.22.0 executed the OpenClaw production TypeScript sources from this worktree with a clean local OpenClaw config (`OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91208-evidence/clean-openclaw-config.json`) and temporary real session store files under `/tmp`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91208
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91208-evidence/clean-openclaw-config.json \
  node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91208-evidence/latest-head-session-extension-proof.ts \
  > /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91208-evidence/latest-head-session-extension-proof.log 2>&1

PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false \
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  src/plugins/runtime.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server-runtime-state.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts \
  src/plugins/runtime/standalone-runtime-registry-loader.test.ts \
  > /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91208-evidence/latest-head-focused-vitest.log 2>&1
```

**Evidence after fix:**

`latest-head-session-extension-proof.log`:

```json
{
  "head": "cbb672ba60134113424ab37b648840bfbb3b655a",
  "proofScope": "production plugin runtime registry surface, host hook patch path, real session store persistence, and projection path",
  "nonEmptyPinnedStartupRegistry": {
    "activeSurfaceIsStartupRegistry": true,
    "activeSurfaceExtensions": [
      "startup-proof-plugin/workflow"
    ],
    "activeRegistryWasChurnedToEmpty": true,
    "patchResult": {
      "ok": true,
      "key": "agent:main:main",
      "value": {
        "state": "after-active-registry-churn"
      }
    },
    "storedValue": {
      "state": "after-active-registry-churn"
    },
    "projections": [
      {
        "pluginId": "startup-proof-plugin",
        "namespace": "workflow",
        "value": {
          "state": "after-active-registry-churn"
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  "emptyPinnedStartupRegistryAuthoritative": {
    "activeSurfaceIsEmptyStartupRegistry": true,
    "activeSurfaceExtensions": [],
    "laterActiveRegistryExtensions": [
      "later-active-plugin/presence"
    ],
    "versionStayedPinnedAcrossChurn": true,
    "patchResult": {
      "ok": false,
      "error": "unknown plugin session extension: later-active-plugin/presence"
    },
    "storeHasLaterActivePluginState": false,
    "projections": []
  },
  "releaseBoundary": {
    "beforeReleaseWasStartupRegistry": true,
    "afterReleaseFallsBackToActiveRegistry": true,
    "afterReleaseExtensions": [
      "later-active-plugin/presence"
    ]
  }
}
```

`latest-head-focused-vitest.log`:

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts

 Test Files  4 passed (4)
      Tests  32 passed (32)
   Duration  32.01s

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.plugins.config.ts

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  23 passed (23)
   Duration  5.18s

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 68.67s
```

**Observed result after fix:** With a non-empty startup session-extension registry pinned, active registry churn to an empty registry did not break `patchPluginSessionExtension`: the patch returned `ok: true`, persisted `{ state: "after-active-registry-churn" }` into the real session store, and projection returned the startup extension. With an empty pinned startup session-extension registry, a later active registry containing `later-active-plugin/presence` did not leak into the session-extension surface: the patch was rejected as `unknown plugin session extension`, no later-active plugin state was persisted, and projection stayed empty. Releasing the pin returned the session-extension surface to the active registry, covering the process-global ownership boundary.

**What was not tested:** A long-running HTTP/WebSocket gateway listener, an external third-party plugin bundle, and live external channel transport were not started in this local Linux proof. The local proof exercises the production plugin runtime registry surface, host hook patch path, real session store persistence, and projection path in-process; focused Vitest additionally covers the WebSocket `sessions.pluginPatch` regression test and gateway runtime cleanup behavior.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** Addressed with latest-head proof at `cbb672ba60134113424ab37b648840bfbb3b655a`, including the final empty-pinned repair. The proof shows an empty pinned startup registry remains authoritative over a later active registry with session extensions.
- **RF-002:** Addressed with local proof and focused tests for the process-global registry owner boundary. The proof covers non-empty pinned startup survival across active churn, empty pinned authority, and release back to the active registry.
- **RF-003:** Addressed with redacted current-head terminal/log proof for the latest authoritative-empty registry behavior. The proof shows `patchResult.ok: false`, no later-active plugin state persisted, and no projection leaked from the later active registry.
- **RF-004:** No repair job is needed for a narrow code defect beyond this PR. The remaining contributor-owned blocker was latest-head real behavior proof and clear disclosure of the local/live proof boundary; this PR body now provides both.

## Current CI status

Previous PR #91208 remote CI failures were attributed to the then-current main baseline rather than this gateway/plugin registry diff: the failing logs converged on `src/agents/sessions/sdk.ts(279,19): error TS2322: Type 'Model<Api>' is not assignable to type 'ThinkingCatalogEntry'.` Main has since recovered at `79d7defd0ba434c32b705ca01316acf50aabdb1b` with no failing check runs, so this PR branch was updated to latest main via GitHub update-branch. At PR head `cbb672ba60134113424ab37b648840bfbb3b655a`, remote checks are rerunning with no failures observed at body-update time.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/plugins/runtime.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-runtime-state.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts`, `src/plugins/runtime/standalone-runtime-registry-loader.test.ts`
- **Scenario locked in:** a startup registry with `sessionExtensions` remains available after `setActivePluginRegistry(createEmptyPluginRegistry())`; an empty pinned startup session-extension registry remains authoritative over later active extensions; `sessions.pluginPatch` succeeds through the pinned registry; gateway runtime cleanup releases a post-bootstrap session-extension repin.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** the tests cover the owner runtime surface, gateway lifecycle cleanup, standalone registry loader pinning, and the production patch/projection path without adding unrelated plugin SDK or protocol changes.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** session-state, compatibility, availability, message-delivery.
- **Risk explanation:** session-state applies because the change routes plugin-owned session extension state through a newly pinned registry surface. Compatibility risk is low because public plugin registration, gateway protocol params, config defaults, and session store fields are not changed. Availability/message-delivery were considered because gateway runtime/bootstrap files are touched; this patch only pins registry references and does not change message dispatch, provider routing, or channel delivery behavior.
- **Why acceptable:** the diff mirrors the existing HTTP-route/channel registry lifetime pattern, includes explicit authority for empty pinned session-extension registries, and is covered by local proof plus targeted regression tests. Related open PR / competition scan found no overlapping open PRs for this same-contract surface (`public-contract-related-prs.json` reported `related_open_prs: []`).

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** the root lifecycle/state invariant was incomplete: session extension registrations were stored on the boot/startup plugin registry, but `patchPluginSessionExtension()` and projection code read `getActivePluginRegistry().sessionExtensions`, so active registry churn caused the lookup source to diverge from the registry that owned the registered extension contract.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** this fixes the source-of-truth lifecycle boundary because session extensions become a first-class pinned registry surface in `src/plugins/runtime.ts`, and patch/projection resolve through that owner surface instead of masking the symptom with a downstream special case in `sessions.pluginPatch`.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High
- **Patch quality notes:** Patch quality warning reviewed: the added test `return undefined` follows the existing `updateSessionStore` callback convention and is not a production default/fallback. No guard/catch/default fallback is added to mask the bug; lookup is moved to the registry lifetime owner.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth owner is `src/plugins/runtime.ts` registry surface state. Gateway startup/reload pins the session-extension surface in the same lifecycle as plugin HTTP routes and channel registries, while `src/plugins/host-hook-state.ts` consumes that surface for session extension patch/projection. Public contract scope is canonical and narrow: no SDK/API/protocol/schema/default migration changed, and external plugin bundle behavior remains out of scope.
target.author: mushuiyu886
